originalPrompt: Educational physics lab station poster, white background, arranged as a 2-wide 3-tall grid of 6 numbered panels. Each panel shows a simple flat illustration of a student action with balloons that have NOT YET been rubbed (so the balloons are plain red with NO dots or charge marks on them at all). Panel order matters — draw exactly as described: Panel 1: a hand rubbing a red balloon with a crumpled translucent clear plastic grocery bag (thin, wrinkly, bag shape clearly visible). Panel 2: a hand rubbing a red balloon with a rigid flat rectangular piece of CLEAR transparent plastic (like a clear plastic ruler or transparency sheet — clearly a solid flat piece with visible straight edges, not a bag, not fabric). Panel 3: a hand rubbing a red balloon with a piece of gray fur. Panel 4: a hand rubbing a red balloon against brown human hair. Panel 5: two hands holding two red balloons, each being rubbed with a piece of gray fur. Panel 6: two hands holding two red balloons, one rubbed with gray fur and the other rubbed with a rigid flat clear piece of plastic. Each panel labeled with a large black number 1–6 in the top-left corner, in reading order (panel 1 top-left, panel 2 top-right, panel 3 middle-left, panel 4 middle-right, panel 5 bottom-left, panel 6 bottom-right). Important: balloons must be plain with NO ion dots or charge symbols drawn on them. The clear rigid plastic piece in panels 2 and 6 must be visibly a solid flat rectangle (with thin outline so it is recognizable as an object), distinctly different from the crumpled plastic bag in panel 1. Flat, friendly textbook style, no shading, no 3D.

originalPrompt: Educational physics diagram, white background, four prediction scenarios in a 2x2 grid, panels labeled A, B, C, D in the top-left corner of each panel. Each panel shows two inflated party balloons suspended from a horizontal ceiling rail by strings. CRITICAL ORIENTATION: each balloon must be drawn the way a real balloon hangs — the balloon's tied KNOT is at the TOP of the balloon (where the string attaches), and the rounded bulbous part of the balloon hangs DOWN below. Do not draw the knot at the bottom. Each balloon has a small white highlight to indicate shiny rubber. Panel A: two light blue balloons, each with a label 'rubbed with plastic'. Panel B: left balloon light blue, labeled 'rubbed with plastic'; right balloon gray, labeled 'rubbed with fur'. Panel C: two gray balloons, each labeled 'rubbed with fur'. Panel D: left balloon light blue, labeled 'untouched / neutral'; right balloon gray, labeled 'rubbed with fur'. In all panels the balloons hang straight down, not interacting yet — students will predict attract/repel/nothing. Flat textbook illustration style, clean lines, no shading, no 3D.

originalPrompt: Educational physics illustration on a white background. Two colored inflated party balloons hang from strings near each other. Balloons are drawn with the tied knot at the TOP where the string attaches, and the round part hanging down (correct real-world orientation). Left balloon is orange and labeled 'rubbed with clear plastic piece'; a rigid flat rectangular piece of CLEAR transparent plastic (like a clear plastic ruler or transparency sheet — solid, flat, with visible thin outline showing straight edges) is drawn nearby on the ground/table level. Right balloon is pink and labeled 'rubbed with clear plastic bag'; a crumpled translucent clear plastic grocery bag (thin, wrinkly, bag-shaped) is drawn nearby. The two plastic objects must be visually distinguishable: the piece is a solid flat rectangle, the bag is a crumpled thin handled bag. The two balloons are shown in neutral positions (students must predict attract/repel/nothing). Flat textbook style, clean lines, no shading, no 3D.
